meta.whyDifferent: Fury is a conjured junk card (Anger+take 1+draw). This is a deck-buildable 0-cost attack whose self-damage line is the point: it flips Frenzy on demand and feeds every take-damage payoff in the pack (Ledger of Vitae, Bleeding self-tick loops).
meta.intendedDeck: Any blood/Frenzy list wanting a free enabler; with own Bleeding stacks the take-1 triggers extra ticks, which Ledger of Vitae converts to Souls.
meta.powerNotes: Cost-0 common baseline is 2-3 dmg; 2 dmg + 1 self-damage is at/below curve, upside is purely the enabler text.

cards.name: Blood Retainer
cards.description: Deal 5 damage. Restore 1 Durability of your Equipment.

meta.whyDifferent: Field Repairs is defensive utility (3 Armor, repair gated behind Frenzy). This puts repair on an attack chassis and prices it in blood, unconditionally: the life cost IS the maintenance fee, and it self-enables Frenzy for the rest of the turn. Bridges blood -> equipment charges.
meta.intendedDeck: Equipment decks running Tithe Censer / Soul Dynamo / Exsanguine Engine; keeps engines running while pressuring and turning Frenzy on.
meta.powerNotes: 1 STR + 1 Life ~ 1.5 energy at the derived 2-Life-per-energy rate; 5 dmg is under the ~6 curve for that, the gap pays for repair:1 (Field Repairs sells conditional repair-all + 3 Armor for 1 energy at Uncommon).

meta.whyDifferent: Same chassis (2-Life common, zero energy) but the opposite decision: Vile Blast is a conditional affliction-scaling nuke; this is flat tempo damage that banks Souls, converting HP into the revive/spend battery every time it is cast. First common that produces Souls from a pure blood cost.
meta.intendedDeck: Souls-greed blood decks: pays HP in, gets damage + Souls out; Souls later fund Usurer's Bargain / Soul Dynamo; the life cost flips Frenzy and feeds Sanguine Tally.
meta.powerNotes: 2 Life ~ 1 energy => 4 dmg is exactly on the common curve; the 2-Souls rider (~1-2 HP of value, Part V revive math) is the compensation for paying in HP instead of energy.

cards.name: Debt Collector
cards.description: Inflict 2 Bleeding. Gain Souls equal to the foe's Bleeding.

meta.whyDifferent: Soulbolt spends Souls for a copy; no shipped card reads the foe's affliction state to GAIN Souls. This turns enemy Bleeding into a soul bank - a payoff bleed decks never had, and a cross-pack handshake with the VenomousLegacy attrition cluster.
meta.intendedDeck: Bleed/attrition shells splashing souls: stack Bleeding, then collect; wants to be played the turn Bleeding peaks.
meta.powerNotes: 1 Life ~ 0.5 energy; 2 Bleeding ~ 2 delayed dmg is on rate. The souls line scales with setup (typically 3-8 Souls ~ 1-2 energy of battery value per Soultap's 1-souls-per-1-HP rate) - conditional value per spec section 4.

cards.name: Sanguine Tally
cards.description: This combat, whenever you spend BLOOD, add a tally. Your attacks deal +1 damage per tally.

enchantment.text: Whenever you spend BLOOD, add a tally. Attacks deal +1 damage per tally.

meta.whyDifferent: Identical enabler (SpendEnergy + EnergyType Is BLOOD accumulator, Bloodbank's exact idiom) but the opposite payoff axis: Bloodbank refunds blood as sustain (heal/draw); this converts it into permanent outgoing damage via the Acquire_Target adddamage:[[enchantmentStacks]] pattern. Blood aggro finally has a scaling reason to keep paying life.
meta.intendedDeck: All-in blood beatdown: Exsanguine Engine / Overdraft-style conversion floods BLOOD payments, each orb ratchets every later hit.
meta.powerNotes: 1 STR + 1 Life for a ramping enchantment; ~2-3 stacks/turn in a dedicated deck ~ Anger-like growth but gated behind actually paying HP. Its own life cost starts the tally at 1.

cards.name: Tithe Censer
cards.description: Whenever you spend BLOOD, restore 3 HEALTH and this loses 1 Durability.

enchantment.text: Whenever you spend BLOOD, restore 3 HEALTH and lose 1 Durability.

meta.whyDifferent: Blood Levy TAXES blood payment (1 Bleeding per BLOOD paid); this is its inverse - a durability battery that refunds the first 3 life payments at 3 HP each. No shipped Equipment reacts to the BLOOD spend event; durability-as-charge-counter for a passive reactive equipment is a new mode.
meta.intendedDeck: Any costLife deck: makes the first blood payments of a fight near-free, then Blood Retainer / Field Repairs re-arm it. Turns repair effects into heal-batteries.
meta.powerNotes: 1 energy -> up to 9 HP refunded over the combat, but only while paying life (each proc needs ~1+ HP spent). Comparable to Bloodbank's 1-heal-per-blood at Legendary-adjacent scale, throttled by 3 charges.

cards.name: Usurer's Bargain
cards.description: Spend 12 Souls: Draw 2 cards and gain 4 Barrier.

meta.whyDifferent: Shipped souls-spenders buy damage (Damnation), kills (Dark End), performance (Mojo) or crits (Reave). Nothing lets you liquidate the revive battery for raw tempo - draw + Barrier at 0 energy. New decision: is 12% of your extra life worth a turn of gas?
meta.intendedDeck: Souls-generating shells (Flesh Installment, Debt Collector, Ledger of Vitae) that bank far past 100 souls, or greed decks that would rather cash out than sit on a revive.
meta.powerNotes: 0-cost gated by a real resource: 12 Souls ~ 12 HP of battery (Soultap rate) buys ~2 energy of effect (draw 2 ~ 1E, Barrier 4 ~ 1E). The play condition uses the shipped [[souls]]:N target format (Mojo/Reave).

cards.name: Ledger of Vitae
cards.description: This combat, whenever you take damage during your turn, gain that many Souls.

enchantment.text: Taking damage during your turn grants that many Souls.

meta.whyDifferent: Soultap is the one-shot version (take N, gain N souls, once). This engine-izes the conversion and widens it to EVERY on-turn self-damage source: life costs, Paper Cut, Chalice of Blood, Zeal upkeep, own Bleeding ticks. The turn-gate (EnemyTurn IsNot, Levy Pain's exact idiom) keeps enemy hits from feeding it - you only profit from wounds you chose.
meta.intendedDeck: The pack's spine: blood decks double-dip every HP paid (damage value + soul value), racing toward the 100-soul revive or Usurer's/Dynamo spends.
meta.powerNotes: Dedicated lists self-inflict ~5-8/turn -> 5-8 Souls/turn (~1 energy/turn of battery at the Soultap 1:1 rate). Costs 1 energy + 1 Life and a card; Rare per engine precedent (Soultap is Rare for the one-shot).

cards.name: Soul Dynamo
cards.description: Use: Spend 8 Souls to gain 2 INT energy and draw a card.

enchantment.text: Use: Spend 8 Souls to gain 2 INT energy and draw a card.

meta.whyDifferent: The Grimoires are activated equipment engines priced in HP (costLife) that pay out cards. This inverts the fuel line: it is priced in energy but BURNS SOULS per activation - the first souls->energy conversion in the pool, on the shipped activate/disable equipment skeleton (Umbral Armor idiom).
meta.intendedDeck: Souls engines (Ledger of Vitae, Flesh Installment) that overproduce; converts banked souls into ramp turns for expensive INT payoffs.
meta.powerNotes: Per use: -8 Souls (~8 HP battery) -> 2 energy + 1 card (~2.5E). 3 uses max, gated behind actually holding 8+ Souls. Energy gained at cap 8 cycles the oldest orb (Part I) - no overflow abuse.

cards.name: Vengeful Mainspring
cards.description: This combat, whenever you spend a Charge, deal 3 arcane damage to the foe.

enchantment.text: Spending a Charge deals 3 arcane damage to the foe.

meta.whyDifferent: Bomb is the only shipped SpendCharge listener and it pays out cost reduction on itself, from hand. This is the first enchantment payoff for the charge economy: every Charge spent from hand-cards (Blood Ritual, bombs, charge items) becomes reach damage. Creates a deck that WANTS to burn durability.
meta.intendedDeck: Charge-dense artifice lists (Blood Ritual, bomb packages, charge items) plus Blood Retainer to reload; pairs with CrimsonLedger equipment for repeated triggers.
meta.powerNotes: 1 energy + 1 Life; ~2-4 charges spent/turn in a dedicated shell -> 6-12 dmg/turn, on par with a Rare engine. UNVERIFIED: SpendCharge scan of the player enchantment zone (only in-hand listeners shipped) - flagged for sim-harness check.

cards.name: Exsanguine Engine
cards.description: Use: Draw a card, then convert its cost to BLOOD.

enchantment.text: Use: Draw a card, then convert its cost to BLOOD.

meta.whyDifferent: Sanguine Casting is a one-shot action (draw highest, convert to blood). This is the repeatable artifact version: 4 durability-limited activations that convert the natural top of your deck, making the whole pack's blood-spend engines (Sanguine Tally, Tithe Censer, Ledger of Vitae) run off any deck. Bridges blood conversion with equipment durability as the throttle.
meta.intendedDeck: The Legendary build-around: energy-poor turns become HP-powered turns; with Ledger of Vitae the converted costs partially refund as Souls.
meta.powerNotes: 3 effective cost (2N + 1 Life) for 4 delayed card+conversion activations; each use ~ Bloodcast (Rare, 1E) minus the conjured-copy upside. Durability cap prevents the infinite-conversion failure mode Overdraft already explores.

meta.whyDifferent: The only weapon with no energy cost: it is paid in Life (1/swing). Every single turn it fires the pack's whole listener stack for free - a life payment counts as damage taken (Frenzy) and as a BLOOD energy spend (Bloodbank-pattern riders) - while banking 1 Soul toward the 100-soul auto-revive. The basic attack IS the ledger: HP out, tempo + souls in.
meta.intendedDeck: CrimsonLedger blood decks (Blood Retainer, Sanguine Tally, Soul Dynamo); frees a full energy per turn for the pack's expensive engines.
meta.powerNotes: Budget at the pack's derived exchange rate (2 Life ~ 1 energy): 1 Life buys ~0.5 energy of value; damage stays at the universal 2 and the soul (~<=1 HP of battery) roughly refunds the life paid, so the real gain is tempo + trigger fuel - text, not stats. Sustained cost ~1 HP/turn is a genuine drawback no shipped weapon carries.

weaponPowers.name: Bloodtithe Weapon
weaponPowers.description: Use your weapon power to take damage equal to your level ([[myLevel]]) and gain twice as much gold.

meta.nearestExisting: Reckless Weapon (cd1: takedamage:[[myLevel]] for statuses); Jewelled Weapon (cd3: gold:[[myLevel]] + potion delve)
meta.whyDifferent: The shipped pool converts HP into combat stats (Reckless) and cooldowns into gold (Jewelled); nothing converts HP into economy. Bloodtithe is the pack's thesis as a button - your health bar is a bank account.
meta.intendedDeck: CrimsonLedger greed lines: fund Tithe Censer/merchant buys; Debt Collector and campfires refinance the HP.
meta.powerNotes: cd2 curve point: Jewelled nets ~lvl gold + a potion per 3 combats; Bloodtithe nets 2xlvl gold per 2 combats MINUS lvl HP (exploration damage buys no combat trigger value, so the HP is a real price). Per-combat gold rate matches Jewelled with the surplus paid for in HP at the pack's 1 HP ~ 1 gold-ish margin. Both codeLines are shipped tier-0 verbatim (Reckless/Jewelled).

weaponPowers.name: Soulscribe Weapon
weaponPowers.description: Use your weapon power to start next combat with Armor equal to twice your level ([[myLevel]]*2). Passive: Whenever you pay a Life cost, gain 1 Soul.

meta.nearestExisting: Fortified Weapon (cd2: Armor [[myLevel]]*3 + card); Bloodbank.json (the rider's exact SpendEnergy/BLOOD listener)
meta.whyDifferent: First tier-0 talent that listens to BLOOD energy spends - it turns every life cost in the pack into soul income (the 100-soul revive as a long-run dividend). The armor half only covers the self-harm the passive encourages.
meta.intendedDeck: Blood-dense CrimsonLedger decks (Flesh Installment, Ledger of Vitae, Bloodprice Falchion) - each blood play scribes a soul.
meta.powerNotes: cd3 curve point: armor package runs UNDER the shipped cd2 baseline (2xlvl vs Fortified's 3xlvl + card) one cooldown step higher; the delta pays for the permanent economy rider, priced like shipped small riders (1 resource per trigger event - per SpendEnergy event, not per HP, so big life costs don't multiply it). Rider encoding is Bloodbank.json's shipped condition (EnergyType Is BLOOD) on a shipped trigger.

weaponPowers.name: Pawnbroker's Weapon
weaponPowers.description: Use your weapon power to pawn a card: destroy a card in your deck and gain gold equal to twice your level ([[myLevel]]*2).

meta.nearestExisting: Voracious Weapon (cd5: destroy a card + heal-5-on-destroy passive)
meta.whyDifferent: Voracious converts thinning into sustain; Pawnbroker converts it into purchasing power - the ledger's last entry: when you run out of blood and souls, sell the deck itself. Distinct payoff for the identical setup action.
meta.intendedDeck: Lean sacrifice decks that want both a thin list and merchant funding for Exsanguine Engine/Soul Dynamo.
meta.powerNotes: cd5 curve point: card destruction is the shipped cd5 effect (Voracious). Gold 2xlvl one-shot vs Voracious' repeatable heal-on-destroy passive (which also triggers off other destroy effects) - at worst equal value. Both codeLines shipped verbatim (remove per Voracious, gold per Jewelled/Bloodtithe).
